name: Ojos de Moya River Gorge Trail
name_original: Hoz del río Ojos de Moya – circular desde Garabella (PR-CU 110)
description: Hike the easy 3.9-mile Riverbed of the Ojos de Moya – circular from Garabella (PR-CU 110) trail, following the tranquil river.
region_intro: Hiking around Garaballa offers diverse outdoor experiences within the Serranía Baja region of Cuenca, Spain. The landscape is characterized by extensive pine forests, deep canyons, and winding rivers, particularly the Moya River. Hikers can explore impressive ravines and gorges, with trails often following tranquil riverbeds or ascending to panoramic viewpoints. This area provides a varied terrain suitable for different skill levels. Best hiking trails around Garaballa The most popular hiking route is Ojos de Moya River Gorge Trail, a 3.9 miles (6.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 41 minutes to complete. This easy path follows the tranquil riverbed through a scenic gorge. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Sierra Trail, a moderate 7.4 miles (11.9 km) path. This route features impressive viewpoints and leads through forested sections. Local hikers also love the Pico Ranera loop from Casa Polan, a 7.0 miles (11.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 3 hours 40 minutes. Hiking around Garaballa is defined by extensive pine forests, deep canyons, and winding rivers. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside strolls to moderate climbs with panoramic views. The routes in Garaballa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 30 hikers have used komoot to explore Garaballa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails are available in Garaballa?
A: Garaballa offers a diverse range of hiking trails within the Serranía Baja region. You'll find paths that follow tranquil riverbeds, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013669281/ojos-de-moya-river-gorge-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos de Moya River Gorge Trail&lt;/a&gt;, as well as routes that ascend through extensive pine forests to panoramic viewpoints. The area features both easy strolls and moderate climbs, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Garaballa?
A: Yes, Garaballa has several easy trails suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013669281/ojos-de-moya-river-gorge-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos de Moya River Gorge Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy path following a tranquil riverbed, perfect for a family outing. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38172736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Olla Recreation Area loop from La Olla&lt;/a&gt; offers a short, easy walk around a pleasant recreational spot with impressive views.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Garaballa?
A: Garaballa's natural landscapes, characterized by extensive pine forests and winding rivers, are generally welcoming for dogs. Most trails are suitable for well-behaved dogs on a leash. Always ensure you carry water for your pet and clean up after them to preserve the natural environment.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Garaballa?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes to enjoy. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010289864/la-sierra-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sierra Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.9 km circular route featuring impressive viewpoints. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24538235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Ranera loop from Casa Polan&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied terrain and significant elevation gain. For a shorter option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38172736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Olla Recreation Area loop from La Olla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find the best views or scenic spots while hiking in Garaballa?
A: Garaballa is rich in scenic spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010289864/la-sierra-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sierra Trail&lt;/a&gt; is known for its impressive viewpoints. The Area Recreativa La Olla also provides breathtaking vistas of nearby ravines. You might also seek out the Mirador del Pulpitejo for panoramic views of the surrounding natural beauty.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature rivers or water features?
A: Absolutely. The Moya River, also known as the Ojos de Moya River, is a prominent natural feature. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013669281/ojos-de-moya-river-gorge-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos de Moya River Gorge Trail&lt;/a&gt; follows the tranquil riverbed, offering a scenic route through sickles and gorges formed by the river.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I visit near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore several interesting points. The 16th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5403982" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Tejeda&lt;/a&gt;, now a charming hotel, offers a cultural and historical dimension. The nearby village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Landete&lt;/a&gt; provides practical services and a picturesque main square. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5514444" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Olla Recreation Area&lt;/a&gt; is also a pleasant spot to relax and enjoy the views.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Garaballa?
A: The best time to hike in Garaballa is typically during the spring and autumn months when the weather is mild and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable, while winter offers a different, often quieter, experience, though some trails might be affected by colder conditions.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Garaballa?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Garaballa, with an average score of 3.8 stars. Hikers often praise the diverse and picturesque natural landscapes, particularly the extensive pine forests, deep canyons, and winding rivers. The variety of trails, from tranquil riverbed paths to routes with panoramic views, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any villages or amenities nearby where I can rest or get supplies?
A: Yes, the village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Landete&lt;/a&gt; is nearby and offers practical services and a picturesque main square, making it an ideal spot to begin a route or take a break. The village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5649064" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garaballa&lt;/a&gt; itself also provides local amenities.

Q: How many hiking trails are there to explore in Garaballa?
A: There are 6 distinct hiking routes documented around Garaballa, offering a good variety of experiences. These include easy riverside strolls and moderate climbs with panoramic views, ensuring options for different preferences and fitness levels.
tips: 16th century Trinitarian Monastery transformed into a charming hotel that offers all the comforts.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Riverbed of the Ojos de Moya – circular from Garabella (PR-CU 110) hike?
faqs.answer: This circular hike usually takes around 1 hour and 40 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: While the overall elevation gain is minimal, making it accessible for many, the trail is considered to have a medium difficulty rating due to its varied terrain. Some sections might be more demanding than others, so a good level of fitness is recommended.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like along the Ojos de Moya riverbed?
faqs.answer: The trail meanders alongside the Ojos de Moya River, offering varied ground. While specific details aren't extensively noted, expect a mix of natural paths. The constant presence of the river and surrounding flora defines the landscape.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Riverbed of the Ojos de Moya trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Garaballa, and you should be able to find parking facilities within the village or near the trailhead. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked for local trails.

faqs.question: Is the Riverbed of the Ojos de Moya – circular from Garabella (PR-CU 110) trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: What wildlife and plants might I encounter on the hike?
faqs.answer: Along the riverbed, you might spot diverse birdlife and potentially even deer among the trees. The area is rich in local flora, and the broader Moya region is known for its lush laurel forests.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, so an early start is advisable, while winter might bring cooler conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary feature is the Ojos de Moya River itself, with its clear waters and natural sickle shape. While not directly on this specific route, the nearby Monasterio de Tejeda is a significant historical site in the region.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route fully covers the 'Hoz del río Ojos de Moya. Garaballa' trail. It also briefly intersects with sections of the 'Camino de la Cueva' and 'Camino de la Vera Cruz' trails.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to access the Riverbed of the Ojos de Moya – circular from Garabella (PR-CU 110) trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities near the trailhead in Garaballa?
faqs.answer: Garaballa is a small village, and while specific amenities like cafes or pubs directly at the trailhead might be limited, you can usually find basic services within the village itself. It's always a good idea to bring your own water and snacks.
